LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S GENERAL AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED FROM NOV-14-2007 TO DEC-10-2007, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302(TO BE HEARD WITH OTHER DEMURRERS). OPPOSING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES WITH COVER LETTER REFLECTING NEW DATE. COUNSEL TO GIVE NOTICE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CLARIFY THE EMPLOYMENT STATUS OF RICHARD MENDOZA. GIVEN THE UNIQUE STATUTES AT ISSUE, CLARIFICATION IS WARRANTED. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING A CLAIM OF LIABILITY ON BEHALF OF A SHORE-BASED WORKER. IN THE 7TH THROUGH THE 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST CLARIFY MR. MENDOZA'S EMPLOYMENT STATUS. GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT ARISE UNTIL THE INJURY IS MANIFESTED, IT WOULD APPEAR THERE IS NO BASIS TO AMEND THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE 10TH AND 11TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE PRESUMED ON THE PRECEEDING CAUSES OF ACTION 6-9. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

AND NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT REFLECT THE HOLDINGS OF HOWLETT V BIRKDALE SHIPPING CO. (1994) 512 U.S. 92, 98 WHICH DEFINES THE CIRCUMSTANCES UNDER WHICH A SHORE BASED WORKER CAN BRING SUIT. THE COMPLAINT IS VAGUE, AMBIGUOUS AND UNCERTAIN AS TO JUST WHAT PLAINTIFF IS CLAIMING IN THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THE VESSELS WERE UNFIT FOR THEIR INTENDED PURPOSE, AND IN THE EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DISTINGUISH BETWEEN SHORE BASED WORKERS AND SEAMAN. AS TO THE NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION (LOSS OF CONSORTIUM), COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER BASED ON THE UNDERLYING CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES, LTD., CROWLEY MARITIME CORPORATION, MATSON NAVIGATION COMPANY, INC., MAERSK, INC. AND NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORP.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS PREVIOUSLY FILED A MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, WHICH WAS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND ON DECEMBER 22, 2008. JUDGE MAHONEY'S JANUARY 28, 2009 ORDER OVERRULING DEFENDANTS' DEMURRERS DID NOT ALLOW DEFENDANTS TO FILE ANOTHER MOTION TO STRIKE. JUDGE MAHONEY'S ORDER STATED ONLY THAT DEFENDANTS SHOULD HAVE ADDRESSED THE EXTRANEOUS ALLEGATIONS IN THE MOTION TO STRIKE THAT WAS FILED, NOT IN THE DEMURRERS. JUDGE MAHONEY HAS CONFIRMED THAT HE DID NOT INTEND TO ALLOW DEFENDANTS TO BRING ANOTHER MOTION TO STRIKE TO ADDRESS THE EXTRANEOUS ALLEGATIONS THAT WERE NOT INCLUDED IN THE INITIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. THE COURT ALSO, ADDS, DEFENDANT HAS TEN DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. COUNSEL WAIVE NOTICE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: BELDON YEE, CSR #2647

MOTION Notice Of Defendants American President Lines, Ltd., Crowley Maritime Corporation, Matson Navigation Company, Inc., Maersk, Inc. And Nedlloyd Lines (Usa) Corp.¿S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs¿ Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants American President Lines, Ltd., Crowley Maritime Corporation, Matson Navigation Company, Inc., Maersk, Inc. And Nedlloyd Lines (USA) Corp.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Third Amended Complaint. Appearances: Talcott N. Bates, Esq. of Emard Danoff Port Tamulski & Paetzold LLP (415) 227-9455, appearing for Defendants and moving party; Stephen M. Fishback, Esq. of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP, (818) 342-7442. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion to strike is denied. Defendants previously filed a motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' Third Amended Complaint, which was granted without leave to amend on December 22, 2008. Judge Mahoney's January 28, 2009 Order overruling Defendants' Demurrers did not allow Defendants to file another motion to strike. Judge Mahoney's Order stated only that Defendants should have addressed the extraneous allegations in the motion to strike that was filed, not in the Demurrers. Judge Mahoney has confirmed that he did not intend to allow Defendants to bring another motion to strike to address the extraneous allegations that were not included in the initial motion to strike. The Court also, adds, defendant has ten days leave to answer. Counsel waive notice. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR 2647

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES, LTD., CROWLEY MARITIME CORPORATION, MATSON NAVIGATION COMPANY, INC., MAERSK, INC. and NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORP.'S SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Allison J. Fairchild, Esq. of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP 818/342-7442 for pltfs & opposing parties; Talcott N. Bates, Esq. of Emard Danoff Port Tamulski & Paetzold LLP 227-9455 for defts & moving parties; Conte C. Cicala, Esq. of Flynn, Delich & Wise LLP 693-5562 for deft General Steamship Agencies, Inc.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - OVERRULED. DEMURRERS FOR UNCERTAINTY ARE DISFAVORED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES A BREACH OF THE DUTY OF CARE FOR AREAS UNDER DEFENDANTS' CONTROL. (PARAGRAPHS 78-79 & 82). DEFENDANT'S DUTY IS NOT BREACHED BY A FAILURE TO PROVIDE RESPIRATORY EQUIPMENT OR TO PLACE WARNING SIGNS. HOWLETT V. BIRKDALE SHIPPING CO. S.A. (1994) 512 U.S. 92, 98; WHITEHILL V. UNITED STATES LINES (1986) 177 CAL.APP. 3D 1201, 1207. DEFENDANT MUST ADDRESS EXTRANEOUS ALLEGATIONS BY FILING A MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO SUCH ALLEGATIONS IN THE 6TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. Plaintiff to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MAERSK, INC., AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES, LTD., CROWLEY MARITIME CORPORATION, MATSON NAVIGATION COMPANY, INC. AND NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: RETT BERGMARK, ESQUIRE (KELLER FISHBACK, NO PHONE NUMBER LISTED) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. TALCOTT N. BATES, ESQUIRE (EMARD DANOFF PORT TAMULSKI & PAETZOLD, 415.227.9455) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT REFLECT THE HOLDINGS OF HOWLETT V BIRKDALE SHIPPING CO. (1994) 512 U.S. 92, 98 WHICH DEFINES THE CIRCUMSTANCES UNDER WHICH A SHORE BASED WORKER CAN BRING SUIT. THE COMPLAINT IS VAGUE, AMBIGUOUS AND UNCERTAIN AS TO JUST WHAT PLAINTIFF IS CLAIMING IN THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THE VESSELS WERE UNFIT FOR THEIR INTENDED PURPOSE, AND IN THE EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DISTINGUISH BETWEEN SHORE BASED WORKERS AND SEAMAN. AS TO THE NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION (LOSS OF CONSORTIUM), COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER BASED ON THE UNDERLYING CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
